GT LEARN #410 🪜 Split Array Largest Sum HARD Binary Search Greedy
nums= k= exp: 18
📝 Problem Statement
Given an integer array nums and an integer k, split nums into k non-empty contiguous subarrays such that the largest sum among all subarrays is minimized. Return that minimized largest sum.
🧠 Solving Approach
Binary search on the answer. The answer lies between max(nums) (each element alone) and sum(nums) (all in one group). For each candidate mid, greedily check if we can split into ≤ k groups with every group sum ≤ mid.
🎯 Core Concepts & Base Case
Search Space: lo = max(nums) (must fit each element), hi = sum(nums) (one group).
Feasibility (canSplit): Greedily accumulate; when running sum + next > maxSum, start a new group.
Binary search: If feasible at mid → try smaller (hi=mid). Else → try larger (lo=mid+1).
⏱ Time: O(n · log(sum)) 💾 Space: O(1)
🧪 Test Cases (click to load)
CLASSICk=2 split→ 18
nums=[7,2,5,10,8]k=2
SORTEDbalanced split→ 9
nums=[1,2,3,4,5]k=2
K=Neach alone→ 4
nums=[1,4,4]k=3
LARGER14 elements→ 25
nums=[10,5,13,4,8,4,5,11,14,9,16,10,20,8]k=8
Result: —
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization — Binary Search on Answer
0/0
←→R 100%
